reviews.author: Mariana
reviews.text: The pass is beautiful, but the road to it is even better. It’s doable as a one-day trip from Cao Bang (and back). I recommend making a loop: go to the pass via the slightly longer northern route from Cao Bang, closer to the Chinese border, and return via the regular, shorter route suggested by Google Maps. The northern route is truly picturesque. Tips: 1. GPS may lag in the northern areas closer to the border. Just follow the main road. 2. Keep in mind that you may get delayed due to road repairs that are not shown on Google Maps, so don’t plan too tightly. Overall, the road is mostly well paved, with a few sections covered in stones (not great for bikers) and some ongoing repair work. A manual or semi automatic bike recommended.

reviews.author: Rhys Scarrott
reviews.text: Amazing views. It’s about a 15-min hike depending on fitness level. Rewarding views at the top. The downside it the amount of rubbish that is around specially at such an amazing location.

reviews.author: Gerben van der Sande
reviews.text: Amazing pass to drive on, well enough maintained road with great views. Definitely worth your time to stop at the top and walk 10-15 minutes to the viewpoint overlooking the pass. Thanks to my guide from Cao Bang Travel who showed me this place!

reviews.author: Dream
reviews.text: The drive here is absolutely beautiful, and the spot itself is well worth the trip. The climb can get a bit muddy or slippery, so good shoes are a must. To reach the view you’ve seen in pictures, head to the entrance opposite the car park, stone steps will lead you on a mostly flat 20–30 minute walk before the final climb to the top. I’m no hiker, but most of the route is easy-going, with only the last stretch requiring a bit of effort. Near the climb, there’s a friendly guy selling water and other drinks,perfect for a quick refresh before tackling the final ascent. Some cool goats along the way also!

reviews.author: Nga Truong
reviews.text: Spectacular view ! A must in the bucket list for anyone visiting Cao Bang. It took us a while and heavy hiking this breathtaking point but that’s completely worth in the end. We loved it, how beautiful of Cao Bang, of Vietnam 🇻🇳😊😊 No ticket to this, I appreciated the hiking road created which any of travelers can enjoy the majestic of this place as long as you seek for it.

reviews.author: Giang Pham
reviews.text: Entrance is right in front of some street vendor shops (see pic) Definitely not for quitters like myself lmaooo Very dangerous on the way down (see pics and vids) my friend and other trekkers have fallen on their limbs a couple of time. So please consider throughoutly if you ain’t tryna sweat your ass off, breath your lungs off or killing your toes (without hiking shoes) The view is cool and all but it took a 30 minute hike so never again for me!

reviews.author: David Froud
reviews.text: There are 15 corners, with a small shop at the top. To get the photo you have to climb the stairs and path opposite the shop. At the top there was also someone selling drinks. Another 70 m to the top. All up about 1 km which will take about 35 minutes

reviews.author: AMM
reviews.text: Visually looks amazing. So cool. But it's a very new road and is already falling apart, there's a giant hole about a third of the way down and looks like it's going to continue to crumble as I saw 2 sets of rockfalls happen that went onto the road while I was going down. I don't think they did a good job of retaining the rock walls they created. Won't last long.

reviews.author: Monica Tinna
reviews.text: 13 Mar 2024 Amazing mountain pass from start to end it's a thrilling, beautiful dirt road with some paved and tar road! I am a travel agent and traveller You can contact me on my Instagram ID Personal ID: mystry_scrt Business ID: mercijourney

reviews.author: RenOverSea
reviews.text: This Pass is just insane. Coming from Bao Lac and descending into the Valley, it's already started to get very exciting as the cliffs, left & and right, are towering in a vertical vertical manner. And then suddenly, I reached the starting ascend up to the Khau Coc Cha Pass. The 14 switchbacks are chiselled into the almost vertical cliff, and that's when the ride is getting insanely hair rising until you reach the top. As of today, the 4th of February 2025, the road is in almost perfect condition, although I don't recommend riding it on a rainy day, as landslides can happen at any time.

reviews.author: Jude Collins
reviews.text: Very cool mountain pass, be weary of the gravel on the sides of the road. Motorbikes worst nightmare. Other than that great view with a hard hike when it’s hot but very much worth it.

reviews.author: devabrat bindhani
reviews.text: Need to do little hiking on rocky roads to get the amazing view of mountain road pass. It’s always amazing to drive motorbike on mountain roads. Must see place. Can park vehicles near starting point of hiking.

reviews.author: Chuyền Tony
reviews.text: Just amazing pass . It was 14 level zigzag pass the most famous pass for motorbikes trip on the north side of Vietnam. For enjoy the view of this pass after you cross the pass leave your bike at small store on the side of the road then you have to hike up to the hill by small trails cross to small jungle it close to 1 km . It was impressive view point wow it amazing view . The pass closes to 16 km from Bao Lac town and close to 100 km from Cao Bang city

reviews.author: Nguyen Duc Hanh
reviews.text: Short distance mountain pass with many sharp turns (14 or 15). Jul 2024 is still under construction 🏗️🚧 but you can drive through by both 🚗 🚌 or 🏍️ with no problem 😉😄

reviews.author: Leona van Diest
reviews.text: 10/10 incredible viewpoint! The pass is a 40 minute drive from Bao Lac, and the road can be a bit tricky since there are many pebbles and small rocks on the road. Also, there is some construction on the road. The viewpoint itself is free, but you have to pay 10.000 dong to park your motorbike at the little stall at the top of the pass. If you cross the road, there’s a small path of red sand which will lead you to the viewpoint. Just follow the red painted arrows and you will arrive in 20-30 minutes. The path is quite rocky, so keep that in mind when starting the hike. Viewpoint itself is soooo good! You can see all 14 curves and take nice pictures. There are two little girls selling water at the top.

reviews.author: Ralph Tb
reviews.text: What a beautiful pass. Park your scooter at the cafe (10.000 dong) and take the path. About 20-30 minutes up and follow the red arrows. Beautiful view up there

reviews.author: Patigoooool
reviews.text: Beautiful viewpoint. It’s easily reached as the path is in plain sight opposite the two shops. Very honest prices from the shops as well even for a tourist. This is normal in Vietnam though👏🏻🥰 It’s about 25minutes walking to the top so allow plenty of time as Cao Bang is still quite a while away.

reviews.author: Des P
reviews.text: Very much worth the visit - I did it on a scooter which was even better

reviews.author: Anh Nguyen
reviews.text: for cyclists, it isn't as challenging as it looks, the hard part is the climb leading to the pass itself. Great view and virtually no traffic due to it not being a state highway

reviews.author: Chris Caruso
reviews.text: I loved this jaw dropping drive and the hike to top it off! Pull up to the nice lady with the food stand/building at the top and she will direct you to the start of the hike! The drive up the road is dirt and stone but my automatic scooter handled it no problem. Rainy day could be tricky but not impossible!

reviews.author: Martin
reviews.text: 11/10/22 Beautiful day with great company. Always wanted to drive this pass ever since I saw it on google. View point is 1km and well worth the trek. The experience and views motorcycling in Vietnam will never be beaten.

reviews.author: KJW 160
reviews.text: Half of this super steep winding road is still under construction, expect sections to be muddy clay and large loose rocks. Going NORTH towards bao lac goes DOWN the road. Going SOUTH towards Cao Bang goes UP the road. Pray you don't have to go up in the rain, would be challenging! 2nd photo is not mine, just gives you an overview. And as always, vietnamese hold safety very highly and construction machines will not stop regardeless of where you are 🤦‍♂️

reviews.author: Sinhbalotravel
reviews.text: Wonderful scenery. You have to hike a small trail approximately 1km to see the whole steps of Khau cốc chà pass

reviews.author: Trạm Phim của Cọp
reviews.text: It's so stunning. You can see a small road where you climb the moutain to take some photos. Following the red arrows on the rocks.

reviews.author: Allison Da Silva
reviews.text: One of the most beautiful views in northern Vietnam. However, you need to trek up a hill for atheist 45 mins to experience these views. Well worth it.

reviews.author: Miki
reviews.text: Visited in Mar 2025. Driving: We were just passengers so stayed calm to avoid getting car-sick. A driver seemed to be super happy every curve. Hiking to the view point: 20-30 min walk one-way. There was a pop up store to sell drinks. Not hard but each step is high considering Asians leg length. View is stunning.

reviews.author: Chee Chung Lum
reviews.text: Follow the red arrow opposite the road stall (paid parking there). A good work out with magnificent view!

reviews.author: KC
reviews.text: The view was great but I didn't expect the hike🤣

reviews.author: Adina K
reviews.text: Hike up to the view point! 10/10
